purpose: Read-only inventory-line pass required by the seed ledger before any zombie or retire_candidate classification: compare the full governance_tools defense inventory against observed outputs and wiring to make zombie candidates visible.

evidence_corpora: memory/2026-*.md dated canonical memory files (2026-03-16 through 2026-07-06)
repo-wide reference scan for candidate modules (py, md, yml, json, sh, ps1, txt)
method.matching_rule: word-boundary regex on the module name; a name mention proves reachability discussion, not a decision path
discounts: .latest-main/ is a git-tracked snapshot of main; hits inside it were discounted as self-mirroring
ai-governance-framework/ nested directory is git-ignored local residue; hits inside it were discounted
method.candidate_rule: modules with no CI/script/hook wiring, no cross-import, and either zero memory mentions or a last memory mention before 2026-06-01 were escalated to per-module review (40 of 193)

zombie_status_vocabulary.zombie_candidate: No wiring, importer, or recent output evidence; retirement requires the seed's focused-review policy.
zombie_status_vocabulary.ruled_out_wired: A concrete load/call path exists (import, manifest registration, or tool cross-reference).
zombie_status_vocabulary.ruled_out_documented_surface: A maintained operator/consumer doc wires the module as an entrypoint; rare use is expected.
zombie_status_vocabulary.ruled_out_operator_surface: Evidence of real manual operator invocations exists (permission allowlist, hook output guidance).
zombie_status_vocabulary.dormant_frozen_line: Belongs to an explicitly frozen line (learning-loop Gate 3, Enumd pause); absence of output is expected and proves nothing.
zombie_status_vocabulary.dormant_design_line: Belongs to an active design/consolidation cluster that is not currently executing code.

claim_ceiling: read-only inventory pass; no defense is retired, merged, downgraded, or changed by this artifact
name-mention scanning proves reference existence, not decision effect and not execution
zombie_candidate means candidate only; the seed's rare_critical policy and focused review are still required before retirement
the 153 modules not listed here have wiring, importer, or post-2026-06-01 memory evidence; that rules out zombie status but does not prove they change decisions

governance docs/protocol layer was not re-classified here; docs/governance/design-note-classification.json remains the doc-layer inventory and governance/*.md protocol docs are a declared residual for a future pass

entries.rationale: A/B evidence line dormant since 2026-05-12; docs and 2026-07-05 ZoneTruth verification artifacts mention it but no wiring or importer exists.

scan_evidence.focused_review: 2026-07-08 read-only review confirmed no active references outside inventory/queue, no dedicated tests, existing apply report run_count=0/replacements=0/write_applied=false, and current backfill data remains insufficient_data/null.

entries.rationale: A/B cost backfill apply tool is a one-off writer for the 2026-05-12 scalar telemetry gap. Historical docs and apply report remain provenance, but active cost observation is covered by ab_cost_hygiene.py and ab_cost_parity_audit.py.

resolution.next_step: Remove or archive the tool in a later reviewed slice if no new scalar telemetry source is provided.

Imported by integrations/enumd/enumd_adapter.py and governance_tools/phase3_gate_adoption_audit.py; library surface, not a zombie.

observations.observation: .latest-main/ is a git-tracked snapshot of the repository inside itself; it inflates any repo-wide reference scan and repository size.
observations.rationale: Its consumer (version audit, lock comparison, or none) must be identified in a read-only slice before any removal decision.

observations.observation: A git-ignored nested ai-governance-framework/ directory and a root .coverage file exist as local residue.
observations.rationale: Ignored local residue; no repository behavior depends on it, cleanup is a local hygiene action, not a governance decision.

observations.observation: _classification_governance_smoke.py at repo root references classification_session_summary but is itself not wired to CI, hooks, or scripts.
observations.rationale: The smoke script and the tool it exercises should be reviewed together.

followups.first: Open one focused review slice for the 4 retire_candidate modules (clean_pilot_admissibility, promotion_gate_receipt_smoke, host_agent_memory_sync_signal, r49x4_metric_ranking) applying the seed rare_critical policy checklist before any retirement.
followups.second: Open one read-only cluster review for the ab_cost_evidence cluster (5 modules) and one for external_integrations (linear_integrator, notion_integrator) to decide keep_observe vs retire_candidate.
followups.third: Do not touch dormant_frozen_line modules until their gates (learning-loop Gate 3, Enumd re-probe) reopen.
